title: Versioned Records, Field Provenance, Reconciliation, and Corrections · Runtime Contract: Record Version
summary: The tool emits structs without source spans, parser versions, transformations, rejected values, or lineage and cannot explain or correct a field. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go record version increment in loss-aware auditable Go record model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup.
Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Record Version · Theory Record Version
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5764 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile versioned records, field lineage, rejection ledgers, counts, corrections, and deletions; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Version record schema, semantic meaning, missingness, invariants, serialization, and migration separately from source markup.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-record-version-4dq4bx(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_record_version_4dq4bx\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?sourceFields < 16)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?accountedFields < 15 \|\| accountedFields > sourceFields)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?correctionCases < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?lineageGaps > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Record Version to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Record Version.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using version and record plus specific case evidence.
